findings.evidence: `GroupLike.lean:29` defines `groupLikeSetSpan`, but `GroupLike.lean:94-97` only proves `Module.Finite` for `groupLikeSpan`; Mathlib already has the finite-span API at `Mathlib/RingTheory/Finiteness/Basic.lean:354` (`Module.Finite.span_of_finite`).
findings.fix: Add a theorem/instance such as `groupLikeSetSpan_isFinite (s : Set (GroupLike R C)) (hs : s.Finite) : Module.Finite R (groupLikeSetSpan s).toSubmodule` using `groupLikeSetSpan_toSubmodule` and `Module.Finite.span_of_finite R (hs.image GroupLike.val)`, then make `groupLikeSpan_isFinite` a singleton specialization.
findings.issue: `groupLikeSpan_isFinite` proves only the singleton case even though the file defines `groupLikeSetSpan`; finite generation should be stated for any finite set of group-like elements and the singleton instance derived from it.

summary: The set-span construction is now at the right level, but the finite-generation API is still only exposed for the singleton special case instead of the natural finite set of group-like elements.
